title: Classic Yorkshire Pudding
description: A traditional British dessert originating from Yorkshire, often served with roast beef. These light and fluffy puddings have a delicate flavor, making them a delight to eat with your favorite gravy or sauce.
instructions: In a bowl whisk together flour, salt, and eggs.
In a separate bowl mix together milk and melted butter.
Add the milk mixture to the dry ingredients and mix well.
The batter should be smooth and free of lumps.
Heat a 12-cup muffin tin in the oven for 5 minutes.
Remove the tin from the oven and add a small amount of vegetable oil to each cup.
Pour the batter into each cup, filling them about 1/2 full.
Return the tin to the oven and cook for 20-25 minutes, or until the puddings are golden brown.
Serve hot with your favorite gravy or sauce.
